content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

July 8, 1992


Steven M. Kamp
901 Wilshire Blvd., Suite 210
Santa Monica, CA 90401

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-92-326

Dear Mr. Kamp:

This letter is in response to your request for advice
regarding the mass mailing provisions of the Political Reform Act
(the "Act")¹ and confirms the telephone advice provided to you
during the course of our telephone conversation on June 3, 1992.

You submitted for our review a memorandum dated June 3, 1992,
regarding sales and excise tax assistance available to riot-
damaged business owners in the Los Angeles area. Upon review of
this memorandum, I advised you that it was in full compliance with
the provisions of Regulation 18901.

Should you have any further questions regarding this matter,
do not hesitate to call me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

[signature]
By: Blanca M. Breeze
Counsel, Legal Division

SH:BMB:bb

___________________________
¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC advised that the submitted memorandum was in full compliance with the provisions of Regulation 18901 regarding mass mailing provisions of the Political Reform Act.

Telephone advice was provided June 3, 1992 and confirmed in this July 8, 1992 letter. Document lacks typical section headers but contains clear advisory content.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether a memorandum regarding sales and excise tax assistance available to riot-damaged business owners in the Los Angeles area complies with the mass mailing provisions of the Political Reform Act and Regulation 18901.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC advised that the submitted memorandum was in full compliance with the provisions of Regulation 18901 regarding mass mailing provisions of the Political Reform Act.
